what_it_does: Defines a structured decision record (a Python dictionary named DECISION) that captures the trade-off between keeping hand-made narrative card visuals, switching to a reusable visual family, or combining both.
observations: The file contains a single DECISION dictionary with no executable code or symbols.
The decision has three options, and the first option is marked recommended: True.
It includes an explanation_source URI pointing to code://build-prep/the-one-application/DECISION-GATHER.md#D2 with a provenance note.
A legibility sub-object provides a plainer name, an 'is' summary, and a 'why' explanation.
standouts: The first option is explicitly recommended without showing comparative criteria.
The explanation_source includes a section anchor and a 'verbatim-verified, section-anchored' provenance note.
The legibility block translates the technical choice into stakeholder-friendly language.
conventions: Decision-as-data pattern: id, meaning, options, scope, subtype, explanation_source, legibility.
File-level docstring states the decision topic and provenance reference.
Provenance is encoded as a code:// URI with fragment.
concerns: The recommended flag is set without visible decision criteria or rationale in the file.
The meaning string is long and partly overlaps the legibility 'why'.
There is no status, owner, date, or deprecation marker.
The file is named .py but contains only a data structure with no code symbols.
notes: This file records a design trade-off about whether decision cards keep bespoke narrative visuals, adopt a reusable family, or layer both, with a recommended hybrid option.
questions: What exactly is 'the reusable family' and where is it defined?
What distinguishes a 'story-style decision' from a 'data-heavy' one in practice?
Which card was approved and by whom?
How was the recommended option chosen—what criteria were used?
What does 'gather D2' refer to beyond the linked URI?
purpose_vs_actual: The docstring states the file is about the hand-made card visuals vs reusable family trade-off (gather D2), and the DECISION dictionary matches that purpose exactly.
apparent_themes: Bespoke narrative vs reusable design-system consistency
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to capture a product/design decision in a reusable, queryable format so future readers can understand the options, the recommended direction, and the source of the decision without regressing the approved card.
novelty: The legibility sub-object that re-expresses the decision in plain language, and the explanation_source as a code:// URI with a verbatim-verified section anchor, are distinctive additions to a simple decision record.
contribution: A structured decision record that preserves the trade-off between hand-made narrative card visuals and a reusable visual family, including a recommended option, alternative implications, and a provenance link to a gather document.
summary_for_embedding: A Python decision record defining the trade-off between keeping hand-made narrative card visuals, adopting a reusable visual family, or layering both, with a recommended hybrid option and provenance to a gather document.
intention_for_embedding: To document and communicate the visual-design choice for decision cards so the approved bespoke card is not unintentionally regressed.
